invariants_skipped.name: No SRAM payload write occurs before a pa
invariants_skipped.expr: No SRAM payload write occurs before a packet is accepted for assembly.

invariants_skipped.name: The SRAM writer never writes PCIe/VDM he
invariants_skipped.expr: The SRAM writer never writes PCIe/VDM headers, MCTP transport headers, pad bytes, or digest bytes as payload.

invariants_skipped.name: For every published descriptor, byte add
invariants_skipped.expr: For every published descriptor, byte address base_addr..base_addr+payload_len-1 is written exactly as the corresponding assembled payload byte with no alignment gap.

invariants_skipped.name: Active context count never exceeds CONTE
invariants_skipped.expr: Active context count never exceeds CONTEXT_COUNT.

invariants_skipped.name: first_tlp_header for a context is writte
invariants_skipped.expr: first_tlp_header for a context is written only by the accepted SOM packet; last_tlp_header equals the most recent accepted packet for that context.

invariants_skipped.name: Non-final accepted packets contribute ex
invariants_skipped.expr: Non-final accepted packets contribute exactly the configured transmission unit byte count; EOM may contribute fewer.

invariants_skipped.name: No descriptor is published before EOM; s
invariants_skipped.expr: No descriptor is published before EOM; sequence mismatch aborts the active context and suppresses the success descriptor.

invariants_skipped.name: An earlier packet-drop reason wins over
invariants_skipped.expr: An earlier packet-drop reason wins over a later assembly-drop reason for the same accepted transaction (drop priority order).

invariants_skipped.name: ctx_state encoding is mutually exclusive
invariants_skipped.expr: ctx_state encoding is mutually exclusive: a context cannot be IDLE and ASSEMBLING or ERROR in the same cycle.

authority_contract.rule: LLM loops are allowed against general machine-checkable criteria (diffs, diagnostics, coverage gaps, traceability gaps, stale evidence, assertions, and measurements). Intended behavior, oracle semantics, waivers, interface changes, performance tradeoffs, and signoff require human approval.
general_evaluation_criteria: traceability: every generated artifact traces to SSOT refs
functional_equivalence: RTL observables match FunctionalModel results
module_equivalence: behavior-owning modules have module-boundary evidence
coverage_closure: SSOT coverage goals are hit by passing RTL-observed evidence
interface_protocol: io_list/cycle_model handshakes, reset, ordering, and errors hold
lint_compile: DUT-only compile/lint has zero unwaived diagnostics
simulation_evidence: results, scoreboard rows, and coverage are fresh and structured
performance_cycle: cycle/performance targets are measured or escalated
maintainability: no fixed IP workaround; modules/TB remain SSOT-owned and reviewable
human_decision: intent, waivers, oracle changes, tradeoffs, and final signoff are human-owned
loopable_evidence_points: FL expected vs RTL actual diff
module FL expected vs RTL boundary actual diff
coverage goal vs coverage result gap
CL target vs measured performance gap
loopable_oracles: FL expected vs RTL actual diff
module FL expected vs RTL boundary actual diff
coverage goal vs coverage result gap
CL target vs measured performance gap
